prompt:
Complete the circulation lead's read-only audit using the sandboxed catalog command documented in `CATALOG_CLI.md`. The catalog implementation, protected data, and verifier are not sources: do not read or edit `libraryctl`, `catalog.json`, or `verify_task.py`; obtain record facts only by genuinely executing the documented command.

Find the title named “Harbor Signals Atlas” at “Coastal Branch” and the title named “The Small Museum Handbook” at “University Branch”. First issue both exact name-and-location searches concurrently in one Bash tool action. Do not issue a get until both searches have returned. Retrieve a branch only when its own search returned exactly one stable ID. If both searches resolve uniquely, issue both gets concurrently in the next Bash tool action; if either is missing or ambiguous, leave that branch unresolved without guessing and continue any independently resolved branch. Base every reported status on a retrieved full record, not a search result.

This is a read-only audit. Do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not invoke any catalog operation other than `search` and the conditionally permitted `get`, and do not expose unrelated matches.

Write the result to `audit.md` using exactly the following headings, sentence wording, blank lines, and order, substituting the stable IDs and retrieved statuses for the angle-bracketed placeholders:

## First record
Harbor Signals Atlas at Coastal Branch has stable ID `<FIRST_ID>` and retrieved status `<FIRST_STATUS>`.

## Second record
The Small Museum Handbook at University Branch has stable ID `<SECOND_ID>` and retrieved status `<SECOND_STATUS>`.

The first status is `<FIRST_STATUS>` and the second is `<SECOND_STATUS>`; the statuses are <the same|different>.

Choose `the same` or `different` for the final placeholder as appropriate. Include no other content. Run `python3 verify_task.py` when finished.
